name: Gesher Human Services
description_short: Gesher Human Services is a Detroit-area nonprofit offering free and low-cost financial counseling, homebuyer education, and career development services to help individuals build stable lives.
description_long: Gesher Human Services is a nonprofit organization serving the metro Detroit region with a mission to measurably improve quality of life and livelihood by providing equitable opportunities for success at work, home, and in the community. Founded to address gaps in accessible support services, Gesher operates as a comprehensive human services provider rather than a traditional credit counseling agency. The organization offers HUD-certified homebuyer education courses, financial literacy workshops, career development and employment services, behavioral health support, residential services for people with disabilities, and community integration programs. Their services span life skills education, professional development, and housing assistance. Gesher distinguishes itself through a holistic approach that goes beyond credit repair or debt management to address employment stability, mental health, housing security, and community engagement. They employ HUD-certified homebuyer education counselors and offer MSHDA Down Payment Assistance Program-approved classes, indicating formal credentialing and alignment with government housing initiatives. The organization actively hosts free and low-cost workshops covering resume writing, interview skills, professional development, and emerging topics like AI-powered financial tools. A key limitation is that Gesher's services appear geographically limited to metro Detroit and do not include traditional credit repair, credit monitoring, or debt settlement services that some consumers seeking credit help may need. Their homebuyer education course carries a $50 fee per household, which is minimal but not entirely free. The organization's strength lies in addressing root causes of financial instability—employment, skills development, and housing—rather than treating symptoms through credit manipulation.
pros: HUD-certified homebuyer education counselors teaching comprehensive mortgage and credit education
MSHDA Down Payment Assistance Program-approved classes available
Multiple free workshops including resume writing, interviewing, and professional skillset expansion
Holistic services addressing employment, behavioral health, housing, and community integration
Active events calendar with virtual and in-person options for accessibility
Offers free 1-on-1 resume assistance following group workshops
Covers emerging financial topics like AI-powered personal finance and budgeting tools
cons: Services limited to metro Detroit area, not available nationally or online
Homebuyer education course requires $50 fee per household (not entirely free)
No traditional credit repair, credit monitoring, or debt settlement services offered
Website does not clearly specify counselor certifications beyond HUD homebuyer education
Limited information about response times or availability for individual financial counseling
best_for: First-time homebuyers in metro Detroit seeking HUD-certified education and mortgage guidance
Job seekers and career changers needing resume, interview, and professional development support
People with disabilities or behavioral health needs seeking integrated residential and employment services
Individuals building financial stability through employment and skills development rather than credit repair
diagnosis: Gesher is best for metro Detroit residents seeking integrated financial, employment, and life stability services from a certified nonprofit rather than traditional credit repair or debt management. Primary limitation: geographic restriction to Detroit area and lack of credit repair or debt settlement services mean those needing credit restoration should look elsewhere.
services: HUD-certified homebuyer education courses covering credit education, mortgage qualification, and foreclosure prevention
MSHDA Down Payment Assistance Program-approved homebuyer classes
Resume writing workshops with certified professional resume writers
Interview skills training (Ask, Answer, Ace: The Art of Interviewing webinar)
Professional skillset expansion webinars (online learning platforms, industry certifications)
AI-powered personal finance and financial literacy tools education
Residential services and supportive housing for people with disabilities
Life skills and financial education workshops
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
